title: Philadelphus maculatus
taxonomy: Philadelphus maculatus is classified as a member of the family Hydrangeaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as hydrangeas and their relatives. Philadelphus maculatus, like its close relatives, is a deciduous shrub that produces fragrant white flowers in the summer months. The species is native to parts of the United States and Mexico. It is also known as the spotted mock orange due to its distinctive appearance.

intro: Philadelphus maculatus, commonly known as the spotted mayapple, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Hydrangeaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in soothing digestive issues and reducing the risk of certain infections. The bioactive constituents of Philadelphus maculatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
